title: Borani Esfenaj (Spinach & Yogurt Dip)
description: A healthy, creamy Persian appetizer featuring sautéed spinach folded into thick yogurt, seasoned with garlic and topped with crunchy walnuts.

instructions.text: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until soft and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 more minute until fragrant.
Add the fresh spinach to the skillet in batches. Cook just until wilted. Season with salt and black pepper. Remove from heat.
Let the spinach mixture cool down completely. If a lot of liquid was released, squeeze the spinach gently or drain it well so it doesn't water down the yogurt.
In a serving bowl, fold the cooled spinach into the plain yogurt. Mix well.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. Garnish with chopped walnuts and an extra drizzle of olive oil before serving.

tips: Squeezing the excess water out of the cooked spinach is the secret to a thick and creamy Borani.
storage_notes: Store in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Give it a stir before serving.
serve_with: Warm flatbread (Lavash or Pita) and fresh vegetable platters.

substitutions.note: Thaw and squeeze completely dry. Skip the cooking step, just sauté the onions and garlic, then mix them in.
Use thick cashew or coconut yogurt for a vegan version.
